review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features relevant to system monitoring. Audit logging and admission controllers are appropriate for monitoring API interactions and enforcing policies. Network policies can be used for monitoring traffic, but the original name was slightly vague. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor adjustment was made for clarity.
naming_improvements: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_enabled to k8s_networkpolicy_traffic_monitoring_enabled to specify the monitoring aspect more clearly
title: System Monitoring - Invoke internal monitoring capabilities or...
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4-c focuses on invoking internal monitoring capabilities. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to enabling audit logging to monitor API server interactions and ensuring retention policies are configured. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce security policies that contribute to monitoring and controlling the cluster's state. NetworkPolicies can be configured to monitor and control network traffic, ensuring only authorized communication paths are used. These functions collectively support the internal monitoring capabilities required by the control.
